content.text: <ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /Users/arjunnair/Desktop/Projects/FOBOH/foboh-project/lib/utils.ts in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>

set the font to Inter. i have it in another app and i like it. please add it

The main font is Inter (from Google Fonts), loaded via next/font/google and applied to <body> in app/client-layout.tsx:14-23.

Note: globals.css:101-102 references --font-geist-sans/--font-geist-mono CSS variables, but those variables aren't actually defined anywhere — so the effective font in use is Inter via the body class.

any specific type of inter font?
Read client-layout.tsx
No — it's just Inter({ subsets: ["latin"] }) with no weight or style specified, so Next.js loads it as a variable font with the default style (normal) and the full weight range available. Individual weights are then applied via Tailwind classes (font-medium, font-bold, etc.) at the component level.

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Two fixes in [ProductSearchFilters.tsx:95-97](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProductSearchFilters.tsx#L95-L97):

- **Left-align / full-width**: added `w-full justify-between` to the trigger so the dropdowns stretch to fill their grid cell (the shadcn default is `w-fit`, which is why they were shrinking and looking off-center).
- **Labels**: the trigger now uses `SelectValue`'s render-children to display the placeholder ("Category" / "Segment" / "Brand") whenever the value is empty or `__all__`, instead of leaking the sentinel. When a real option is picked, it shows that option.
Make it light gray like search and product / SKU says.

content.text: Done — wrapped `product.sku` in `font-mono` in [ProductList.tsx:45](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProductList.tsx#L45). The "SKU" label and UOM stay in the default sans font; only the code itself renders in Geist Mono.
i have a Figma file with the UI componetns and layout and everything. Do you think if i gave you a link for that it woudl help?

Yes, hugely — but with a real caveat about how I can consume it.

**What helps:** exact spacing, colors, type scale, component states, hover/disabled/empty/error variants, and the overall layout intent. Right now I'm guessing at what "good" looks like for this brief; a Figma reference would let me match it.

**The caveat — how I can actually read it:**
- A plain Figma share link → I can't open it. I have no browser/Figma session.
- **Best:** exported PNGs/JPGs of the frames you care about (drop them in the repo or paste paths). I can view images directly and reference them while building. Use `Cmd+Shift+E` in Figma → PNG @2x.
- **Also great:** Figma's "Copy as PNG" on a frame, then drag into the chat — same thing, no file needed.
- **If you want it fully wired:** the Figma MCP server exists and gives me structured access to frames/components/tokens, but it needs setup on your side (Figma desktop app + dev mode + MCP enabled). Worth it if you'll be iterating against the design a lot; overkill for a one-shot review.

For this project I'd start with **screenshots of the key frames** (the new-profile page, the product card, the customer card, any empty/error states). That covers ~90% of what I'd get from the file itself and takes 30 seconds.

Basic Pricing Profile Card
 - In this card, once you click mark is complete, the profile name and its description should update in the card header. The card currently has the basic pricing profile and cheeky little description goes in here as default standard text, which shouldn't be there. The UI is not intuitive is what I'm trying to say. And when your mark is complete, it doesn't update in a way that makes sense. Only the description updates, not the name of the profile.


Select Product Pricing Card
 - In this card, expand the base on dropdown select that says global wholesale price, to be a bit wider so that you can fit the whole name, global wholesale price, inside that dropdown box.

content.text:
All three fixes in. Type-check passes cleanly.

**Basic Pricing Profile** ([ProfileBasicCard.tsx:23-30](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx#L23-L30))
- Header `h2` now shows the entered profile **name** once you mark complete (falls back to "Basic Pricing Profile" otherwise).
- Subtitle shows the entered description when complete, and "Name and describe this pricing profile" as a real helper hint when not — no more dummy placeholder text.
- "Mark as complete" is disabled until a name is typed (otherwise the header would have nothing meaningful to switch to).

**Select Product Pricing** ([AdjustmentControls.tsx:53](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AdjustmentControls.tsx#L53))
- Changed the "Based on" trigger from `max-w-sm` to `w-fit min-w-64`, so it auto-fits "Global Wholesale Price" without truncation.

**Next button** ([SelectProductPricingCard.tsx:228-237, 240-244](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx#L228))
- Added stable `id`s on the basic and assign-customers cards, and wired `Back` / `Next` to smooth-scroll between them. (A "Pricing Completed" state field would be a fuller fix, but the buttons now do the thing users expect — move you to the next section.)
